commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For you are not a God that has pleasure in wickedness: neither shall evil dwell with you. Hebrew implies "in order "as those who neglect prayer must be all in confusion. God enables those who are diligent, to see wonderful things. (Berthier) Iniquity. No: he has it in abhorrence; (Calmet) consequently he cannot be the author of it, but hates those who commit evil, though he loves them as his creatures, and wishes their conversion. (Worthington) On this subject I will meditate. (Menochius).
